item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image could not be sourced reliably and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why test with a dolphin and not a fish?

A marlin would get there just as fast, and no risk of drowning from being so compelled they neglect to come up for air.
Better yet feed a shoe to a great white shark to ensure A) the duct tape won't come off, and B) nothing will eat it on the way.

Nitpick in a similar vein - repositioning instances in containment by hand is a huge unnecessary risk. Do it robotically. Or mount the safe on a swivel so it can be rotated 180o when needed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a lot of this that isn't that great, but it still gave me the heebie-jeebies.

item.title: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded: Just an FYI, I went ahead and changed the audio player from Flash to HTML5, as Flash is rapidly losing support and already can't be viewed by many people, including Firefox and Mobile users. No content changes were made, and the article is otherwise unchanged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a modified version of the Train sound picked up by the hydrophone array. There is no words spoken. Just a low droning pitch interspersed with beeping from the recording device starting to fail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't get the audio log, due to lacking flash. is there a text version?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If it weren't for the creepy-as-hell audio log, I'd never have upvoted this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kind of reminds me of that mysterious phenomenon of severed feet washing up in the Salish Sea… Interesting indeed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Julia and the Bloop have lost their effect on me and just bore me now. My favourite is a tie between Train and Upsweep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know, there's some scarier sounds out there. Like Julia, which in my opinion is the scariest unknown deep sea sound.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed; the audio file is what really did it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't deny that my initial idea was partially inspired by The Vancouver Feet. I tried not to base the article around it though because that would be a little tasteless, yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"oh my god, someone actually wrote an article about anomalous dog s%@t!"

I actually laughed out loud at that and got an angry glare from a room mate who was trying to sleep.

Anyway, when I first looked at the picture, I got the distinct impression that this was inspired by the shoes containing feet (sans rest of the body) that kept washing up on shore in British Columbia a few years back. A tad tasteless (basing an SCP on actual missing people), but a solid idea nonetheless.

The dog shit would make a good -J, though there's already a fecal -J out there.
…
You know, that may be an idea…a lawn where dog poop always appears, no matter the measures to keep dogs out. I don't know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great! I'm glad you enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think those were all good changes and addressed my complaints. Thanks for writing a cool SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, all very helpful points. Tell me if you think these changes work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't love this but it's good enough for an upvote. I do think a few improvements could be made.

"Due to the contagious nature of SCP-1667's effects, no shoes, boots, sandals, flip-flops, slippers, flippers, footwraps, galoshes, mocassins or clogs are permitted to enter a two (2) meter radius surrounding Containment Locker 16 without the prior approval of Researcher ████████. Socks are permitted."

Pet peeve, redacting necessary information in the containment procedures. If you need his approval for something, how are you going to get it if his name is redacted?

"On a fortnightly basis, one (1) member of personnel must carefully open Containment Locker 16 and replace all footwear inside to its most northwesterly positions."

I had to pause and re-read this sentence again because I couldn't tell if it meant repositioning the footwear or replacing it with all new footwear. If you just change "replace" to "reposition", I think it would make the sentence clearer.

"The contagious nature of SCP-1667 was discovered when other articles of footwear brought within an approximately one (1) meter radius of SCP-1667-1"

The area of effect has already been established, so I don't think it's necessary to repeat it. You could just say "was discovered when articles of footwear were brought into SCP-1667-1's area of effect" or "was discovered when articles of footwear were exposed to SCP-1667-1". The area of effect is also inconsistent - here it's 1 meter, in the opening it's 2 meters.

From the test log: "Further investigation should be considered unnecessary however, as it is presumed subject was simply experiencing visual hallucinations at the time, possibly pressure-related."

To me, this is absolutely ridiculous and I almost considered a downvote because of it. We are talking about scientists who specialize in researching paranormal objects which, in some cases, are capable of dooming the human race… and they write something off as a "hallucination". If we were talking about regular researchers, sure, but this is the Foundation. That sort of rank naivety doesn't befit them, in my opinion.

Hope that helps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never underestimate the power of duct tape, my friend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really actually quite liked this one, especially the audio recording. Certainly creepy enough. The one thing I couldn't help thinking, though, was 'How did they get the dolphin to wear the shoe? Does the Foundation even have that kind of technology?'. Nevertheless, good article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An old favorite comes back, and better than before! Good to see this again!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It might just be author favoritism, but the way the last log kept expunging the large whatever-it-was the D-class was seeing made me think of SCP-1128.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really get this SCP. It's a contagion that spreads between shoes and makes people go to a certain spot in the ocean before vanishing. It's somehow related to a giant creature.

Remind me of http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-1409 but I like 1409 a lot more. :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So originally I was reading this article on my phone. The picture is obviously very small on the phone, so while this article's picture more clearly looks like a ratty shoe, on the phone it looked more like a pile of dog feces.

I wasn't really sure and couldn't be bothered to zoom in and figure it out, so I figured I would start reading the article, which would make it clear to me if it really was dog feces or not. Then the article started talking about shoes, and I thought to myself "oh my god, someone actually wrote an article about anomalous dog s%@t!"

Fortunately, not long into the description section that idea was dispelled. However, I would like to say that if anyone would like to create an SCP about anomalous dog poops that get stuck on your shoe, you're more than welcome to the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a bit subtle but it's implied that at least three members of the MTF drowned through "Black combat boot, owner deceased". I'd appreciate any suggestions to improve the expunged parts in general though.

The audio file was just intended as an interesting climax to the article. The last experiment log is supposed to explain the entire point of the SCP's effect, that the victims are being led to a living creature, not just the ocean. Any suggestions to make that seem like less of a stretch?

I agree about the objects being destroyed. "Lost" or "unrecoverable" would be better words to use. Maybe changing the whole article to say that the SCP is partially uncontained would work better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the recovery of SCP-1667-1 from █████ Beach and in the resulting chaos, [DATA EXPUNGED].

This expunged portion felt jarring to me. Almost anything could have occurred in the expunged area, which is why it's better not to expunge the end of paragraphs, as there isn't enough context clues to infer the nature of the events.
There's also a little too much expunged material in the last log, and it's difficult to read when constantly having to go over blanks in the structure.

The audio log didn't add much, and seemed like a desperate tie-in to the SCP about, well, shoes. I won't question the connection between a shoe contagion and the Pacific Ocean, but trying to connect it with a dull audio log and a mysterious underwater creature is both a stretch and a letdown. I also have a problem with the laziness of the Foundation in Log 02/12. They just presume this object is destroyed? It could wash up on a beach in Hawaii or Japan, and then we could have an outbreak on our hands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha, thanks. Sorry the article didn't do much for you but I'm glad the audio file worked. I spent a while editing the unidentified underwater sound recording known as "Train", which the original version scared the shit out of me too. More info about the sound if you're interested.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was bordering on "meh" because the idea didn't really do anything for me, but then I got to that audio file and it scared the living shit out of me. +1, +1 hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really good points, thank you!

I took out the part about the dog being released because I agree it seems too risky. However, I think it's reasonable the Foundation would assume the samples almost 3000km away from land wouldn't wash back up on beaches. I actually think it adds to the creepiness a bit because of the small chance that the contagion could have breached containment without the Foundation's knowledge.
Edit: I updated the SCP to be partially uncontained. Thoughts?

I added to the recovery backstory, tell me what you think.

I also nerfed the effect radius to 2 meters (for simplicity's sake, let's assume the locker is on the ground floor).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really think the Foundation would be willing to risk spreading the effect by letting samples go. Unless the trackers were attatched to the shoes instead of the animals, there's too much of a risk of the objects being washed up on beaches.

Also, the recovery log makes it seem like the foundation heard about a shoe washing up on a beach and immediately sent an MTF. I sounds kind of silly.

Plus, is the 4 meter zone a sphere? Because then they'd probably stop people from using the rooms above and below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't believe I liked this the first time it was posted.

I seem to like it now, though.

But it still reminds me of the Red Dwarf bit about intelligent shoes. (Look it up.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I can see the similarities but I think it's certainly different enough overall. The original version of this article was from April 22nd, a few weeks before SCP-1409 was submitted.

Edit: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/forum/t-468180/scp-1409#post-1447492

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mm, I disagree. Sure, there's one similar component (must go to the location), but everything else differs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has a very similar effect to SCP-1409, although its original form apparently pre-dates that article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I submitted it a few months back when it was half-finished, bad idea. Been though a major rewrite now and I think it should do better.

Edit: Can't wait. Everything helps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I swear I've seen this before.

No vote. Will be back with a critique later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
kicked Researcher ████████ to the groin and sprinted from the room

Probably that "to" was meant to be an "in"?
